title: Who Gets Innocence? Karmelo Anthony, Austin Metcalf, and America’s Racial Judgment
excerpt: The Karmelo Anthony and Austin Metcalf case reveals more than a tragic confrontation. It exposes how America distributes suspicion, innocence, sympathy, and moral complexity along racial lines. Scripture condemns false witness, partial judgment, and judging by appearance.

title: Three Laws of Logic
excerpt: Identity, non-contradiction, and excluded middle as the Bible’s ordinary grammar for truthful speech.

title: Scripture & Logic: Friends, Not Foes

title: Israelites and Hamites: Recovering a Dark-Skinned Legacy

title: Is Truth Reliable?
excerpt: Exploring why truth is reliable — grounded in God’s character, enduring across time, and vital for Israel’s covenant identity.

title: Is Creation Logical?
excerpt: The Bible does not ask you to suspend reason; it invites you to see reality as it is: ordered by God’s wisdom. From the opening cadence of Genesis—God speaks, it is so, it is good—creation is not chaos—it is structure, sequence, and sufficiency.

title: Gentiles
